intro: Sclerorhachis paropamisica, also known as S. pungens or Paropamisic grass, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of S. paropamisica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, S. paropamisica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its dried roots and rhizomes are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for various health purposes.
